name: Hawaiian Marinade
directions: Combine all ingredients in a jar, cover tightly and shake vigorously.
Store in refrigerator until ready to use Shake vigorously before using.

name: Texas-Style Lomi Lomi Salmon Salad
directions: Boil pasta on until done then immediately douse with cold water until slightly chilled.

name: Lanai Pasta Salad
directions: Combine pineapple, reserved juice and remaining ingredients in large bowl; toss to coat.

name: Earon's Hawaiian Plate Lunch Salad
directions: Boil macaroni in water with salt according to package directions and drain.
Immediately, while noodles are still hot, mix in mayonnaise and salad dressing.  After it has cooled, add carrots.
Chill for several hours before serving.  Taste improves overnight.

name: Lana's  Macaroni Salad
directions: Cook elbow macaroni according to package directions, rinse in a colander and let cool.  Mix all ingredients together and let chill for a few hours.

name: Sara's Hawaiian Pizza
directions: Mix cheese together in a small bowl, sprinkle a little on the sauce.
Scatter evenly with the onion, ham, and pineapple.
Bake at 180C for 20 minutes, or until cheese is melted and bubbly, and base is cooked.

name: Mauna Kea Shrimp
directions: You can be authentic and use "real" Japanese Mayonnaise (Kewpie), but good old Hellman's works fine too.The Sriracha hot sauce is the secret ingredient (the one with the rooster on the bottle!).
Mix Sriracha with mayo and paprika. Set aside. Deep fry your shrimp and simply toss the volcano sauce in a mixing bowl with the shrimp.
